title: hibbertia stenophylla
intro: Hibbertia stenophylla, also known as Guinea flower, is a small, low-growing shrub native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Hibbertia stenophylla has been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to promote healing and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or creams, and are often used to support skin health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
